item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So thats why the show is still going on for so long…

+1 gotta love the teenage mutant ninja turtles

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was very good. A little too lighthearted to what it could be o some points, but nonetheless very good.

I actually got the ninjas coming pretty soon, but it didn't bothered me in the slightest. It delivered their supposed powers well, and it makes room for pop culture references, but also for historical references, like how the legends about ninjas came to be and spread in Japan in the last centuries.

On the matter of the TMNT, it could be done a little subtler, but I'm good enough with the statement that cocaine was an ingredient. My headcanon, with the violent turtles of the original comics being anomalies existing for real and the cartoon being one of the first weapons ready for the Foundation makes everything right for me.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Well shucks, this is a great skip. Not a lot of room for crosslinking though. However, the mention of the UIU in the interview log can be turned into an inline link to their hub. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-2928-13: Yeah. Friend of a friend, yadda yadda, UIU threw me at you guys.

To this:

SCP-2928-13: Yeah. Friend of a friend, yadda yadda, UIU threw me at you guys.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn, this is a neat one - can't believe you made that work.

What impresses me the most about this is that even before I got the interview, I understood what was going on perfectly (even if I didn't know it was specifically about ninjas).

My only criticism is that I don't particularly care for, or understand, the TMNT reference at the end.

Well done, though. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really seems more like a -J than a mainlister, and I'm really not one to make that sort of statement lightly.
Hell, I don't think I've actually made any statement on this site in, what, 3 years now?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, when this article says that it tries to prolong the duration of series, it means that it's responsible for all those Naruto filler episodes? So it's not animation/audio designing flaws, but active Foundation interference. I've been waiting for too long so that the anime can end.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I LOVE this! But…
The interview (which is great) leaves it too silly for what I want in a main-list SCP. It is way too good to downvote, but I'm not upvoting. Sorry. No, really, I'm sorry not to upvote it.
In the unlikely event that it is ever voted out, please, PLEASE repost it as a -J, and I will try very hard to upvote more than once.
And I know that the idea is a serious (and good) one, making -J wrong for the piece, but the delivery is just so beautifully silly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pop culture references in an article are generally kind of silly for me, but I like how you're not super sure what they are until you come to the slow realization via the interview log that these are ninjas, then the references fall into place. It's this kind of steady, building realization (even in a humorous way) that I love to see in articles.

item.title: Well....
item.content:encoded: http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-504

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally, I don't see the point for a TMNT reference. However, I like how the production of Naruto effectively nullified SCP-2928's anomalous effects. It makes sense why they had to drag the manga for so many years, but there are more questions to be answered here.

Here's to the most un-ninja-like ninjas of the modern age!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would have upvoted this before those last few lines. +2

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's just too general, you didn't came on a specific subject, also the idea is too big to explain what really happening is. -own opinion

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's room for silly, humorous stuff on the mainlist, of course, but this is a bit too ridiculous. -1.
Even ninjas can be anomalous

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wat. just…wat. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the idea of Naruto being so bad it ruins things for real, anomalous ninjas, and this SCP gives another reason it could still be going after all these years. Damn I hate that series.
Anyway well done. Didn't catch on until the interview but this is now the silliest mainlister I've upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … the most prominent of which is a popular children's cartoon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sheer brilliance.
This made both 10 year old me and current me grin manically.
+!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent!

+1 for TMNT propaganda.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meanwhile the Hashshashin are benefiting hugely from Assassin's Creed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2928-13: I'm a ninja!

And that bit of lolwut? was when I upvoted.

Also for the mental image of a ninja in the interview room. Being interviewed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… wha… turtles… huh?

This is the first SCP, including joke SCPs, to actually make me laugh out loud. The ninja turtle thing was incredibly funny, especially that last line about the creation of the turtles involving a lot of coke, and the comics being a cover-up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Suzuki: 1996. Thank you, that confirms some theories. Can you tell me about the Alpha Phase, the cause of the hallucinations?

Why say that?

The rest of this, I quite like. The interview is fairly funny. Nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love you.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get it, ancient ritual to make someone the ideal ninja backfires when everyone's ideal of "ninja" became anime protagonists and turtles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ninjas, internet meme thing
Ninja Turtles
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't hit the upvote button because I can't see it, the tears of laughter are blurring my vision.

There it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it would be better with no actual direct use of the word 'ninja' or 'ninjutsu'. The line about a Japenese stage hand would be enough to get the joke, and references to 'stealth' and 'hidden arts' and so on. The pirate line is a great example.

Also the turtles bit seemed ridiculous to me, perhaps because I have no real fondness for TMNT (I don't dislike it, I've just never interacted with it). And cocaine doesn't make you creative, or want to do 'weird' things. Unless you meant the turtles were given coke. Which I guess makes sense, given how fighty they are.

E: oh, but I still upvoted, and it's still a great article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think it's supposed to mean they're REAL, just that for a time the ninja who created them did so to reshape the public image of what ninja are. Because they were high.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ninjas? Really? And they're stopped by Naruto and the Ninja Turtles!? And somehow, this article is still awesome?! You sir, have earned your upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is, without a doubt, the stupidist mainlister I've ever upvoted.

So…Kishimoto's under Foundation protection as a collaborator in the KUDZU thing eh?

Then perhaps those rumours about him 'dying' during the 2011 earthquake and tsunami are partly true; the Foundation needed him to combat SCP-2928 so they somehow managed to 'revive' him from the dead, or just protected him 'normally' from rubbles and shit (although Kishimoto was never really in any danger in the 1st place) using Foundation magic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
GO! POWER RANGERS GO!

NINJA STORM LET'S GO!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whose side are the Power Rangers on? I'VE GOT TO KNOW!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, this is great. Nice use of expungement. I was guessing ghosts until the interview. And the interview is great too, the opening line is right to the point, and it ends on a kicker. Very much upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I understand the redacted references correctly, these guys are not, in fact, Naruto-style ninajs. Naruto is the counter-meme that short-circuits their techniques.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Part of me loves this, but I just can't bring myself to upvote. Maybe it's the interview telling us that the Ninja Turtles are real, I don't know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This should not work. This should be a terrible idea, and be downvoted into oblivion in less that 24 hours.

So why did I upvote it?? Probably memetic or something, I guess… ;-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be fair, this particular skip is here of his own volition, and telling the Foundation about the other ninjas is actively helping them protect him. He sought the Foundation out, and he knew what he was in for. Although he chose it, he's still got to deal with the fact that he's essentially a prisoner now. So why not try to make it a little easier to deal with by combating the situation with some humor?

Besides, maybe all this apparent cooperation will make escape easier should he ever choose to do so. He is still a ninja, and he has at least two unknown ninjutsu disciplines up his sleeve.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The interview hurts my view of this one. I liked what I was reading up until that point. Is it just me, or do most interviews on here just seem, way too casual? People cracking jokes, giving up everything they know like they are chatting at a party, just being a "really cool dude."

The SCPs are very chill for being locked away by the Foundation and I really can't take their conversations serious in the slightest. :/

Edit: I also have problem with the fact that ninjas have been depicted in media for a lot longer than 1999, with plenty of bumbling, easily spotted characters coming up before Naruto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of my core tenets as a writer is "There is no such thing as a bad concept, only a bad execution".

This is a concept I would never have imagined could work as an SCP, and you knocked it out of the park.

Upvote no jutsu.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Naruto. Seriously, Naruto ninjas are real-ish. Yeahno. I just can't take this article seriously in any way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ninjas can turn functionally invisible to people who believe that ninjas are supposed to be stealthy. They attempt to spread fiction that depicts them as such, while the Foundation's containment procedures are to spread material that depicts them as being highly visible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Naruto ruins everything

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, it's official, I'm dumb and I'll need some explanation.

Nonetheless it's an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ahahahahahaha
NINJAS
turtles
NEEEEEEEEENJAS
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
whhhhhhhhhhhaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaAAAAAAAAT.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After reading that interview my mind exploded, imploded to revert the explosion, then reexploded. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This should not be good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… have been… waiting for this.

+

1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate myself for upvoting this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So a memetic phenomena used by ninja to make themselves invisible or something, and the teenage mutant ninja turtles are real, at least according to the interviewee.

+1 for making me laugh with that last part by the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You… fucking… you made it work. Fuck you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus fucking Christ, you finally posted this thing so I can finally upvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damnit, ninjas and robopirates on the same day. What is the foundation coming to?

item.title: Author Notes
item.content:encoded:
Blame Randomini.

  
- SURPRISE

NINJAS
